reviews.review_text:
[u][b] BACKGROUND: [/b][/u]

Listen... at the time of this review, I have yet to finish this game. I have yet to kill the big bad... and yet, I felt the uncontrollable urge to leave this review (Just brainwashed cult things I guess).

[u][b] APPEALS: [/b][/u]

While the gameplay aspect of this game isn't new to those of us used to playing dungeon crawler-esque games, Cult of the Lamb (COTL) brings a unique world-building and narrative experience to the genre. Aside from the amazingly crafted dungeons, COTL also has unique non-dungeon puzzles and side quests/mini-games (why did I think I was in Stardew Valley for a moment?). Gameplay is also seamless (I haven't come across a stutter, bug or crash).

That being said, most dungeon crawlers are notoriously difficult. If you're like me (enjoying a good challenge but lost interest/energy when things become too tedious/grindy - hey! I'm a patient person, I swear), then you'll appreciate the difficulty system in COTL. There are three difficulty tiers and you can choose between them at any time. There are no achievements linked to the game difficulty.

While the game mechanics are simple and easy to understand, the game is sufficiently challenging without being suffocating or frustrating. For example, you must manage certain resources to effectively play the game. These resources are hard to acquire in the beginning but eventually it becomes a non-issue (I have yet to finish the game and I've already outgrown resource use - except food). As you progress, resource management becomes easier, and towards the end-game content, resource management is not the issue; gameplay choices and time management are. So there's a bit of healthy pressure, but the game is not tedious or grindy.

The artwork and music are well crafted. COTL is bright, colourful and incredibly adorable with the art style (unique from many games in its genre). The music is upbeat, fun, and it never gets boring even on repeat.

The story and dialogues are well-thought-out and beautifully written. The lore is spread across the dungeons, mini games, and post-game materials but it isn't difficult to obtain (So I hear >.>).

The game uses a day-count feature and it auto-saves frequently. You can also manually save outside of dungeons. Yes, you cannot save in the middle of a dungeon.

[u][b] CONCERNS ABOUT THE GAME: [/b][/u]

Honestly, I cannot even call this a 'concern' and its probably just me being petty - In COTL you have the ability to craft and beautify your base. If you're Speedy McFingers, like myself, and you accidentally press to build something, there is no way to cancel that action, so you lose your resources (you can move structures/decorations around and/or remove them once its already built). 

[u][b] CONCLUSION: [/b][/u]

COTL is such a fun game. I haven't had such great fun in a long time. You should definitely grab it. I'll take your thanks and your devotion later.
Literally added the sex and gun update, not even minecraft could do it

Cult of the Lamb is a cute base building game with roguelike elements. You can customize both your camp and followers while defeating the false prophets. My only complaint is sometimes the combat can feel repetitive but the rest of the game makes up for it.

lf you enjoy games with cute graphics but dark themes, this game is definitely for you. l didn't expect much when l first bought it, but before l knew it, l was opening the game whenever l had even the smallest bit of free time and genuinely felling sad whenever one of my followers died :( Turns out running a cult is way more stressful than it looks.

Not gonna lie I have more hours on my consoles than I do on here that's for sure Very cute game if you like a little bit of gardening a little bit of decorating with a little bit of control over your community that you are building you'll love this also if you love Binding of Isaac especially when it comes to the dungeons it doesn't take much to learn everything and the stories are phenomenal Highly recommend this game for anybody who wants to support a small company and who just enjoys a really good story Can't wait to see what they come up with next

I play very few games, when I opened cult of the lamb I played for two and a half hours straight. within two days I reached twelve hours played. very good game. good mechanics, good story, good sound design, and wonderful art style!
would recomend!
Every single aspect of this game is so well-developed. The creators did not skimp on either the combat or the cult-development.
Fun, addictive, you get to clean your followers mess, you get to clean your followers mess, YOUR FORCED TO CLEAN YOUR FOLLOWERS MESS, and so much more.

Beautiful soundtrack, wonderful art and character designs, and updates that keep the game moving fowards. Absolute love to the developers of this gem.

Chicken sideways face.

I played the game about 5 times. And it is still great. Love the aesthetics! And it feels very cozy even though it’s a little dark. 
11/10 !

This game is one of my favorite games that I have ever played in the past few years. I played it a lot while I played on Xbox and I also bought it on here and started the game fresh again. Overall, I don't see myself quitting this game in the near future and recommend you play this game.
a perfect peak game i want woolhaven dlc it looks peak two recommend that you get the bundle with all i bought the old one before woolhaven

son

this game is too fun
you can have sigeon pex
i really like the doctrines, my favorite being the mind control one

Love it.  Feels like a near perfectly balanced game.  Great building and action gameplay mechanics.  Not normally into this type of game but I enjoyed it so much I am buying the DLC so I can play that one too.
An Amazing game with great action and complex story. 10/10 Would Recommend.
está bueno el juego jeje es muy entretenido cuidar de tu rebaño de seguidores jeje tambien está chistoso matar a los que se oponen a tí jeje

This game is impossibly adorable, addictive, and just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ great. It does everything that it does very well. It is also extremely accessible and customizable so that any difficulty can be surmounted.

I can confidently say that this is one of my favourite games of all time. All Hail the Lamb.

could not recommend this game enough!

for starters, the art style is absolutely beautiful. it's the first thing that drew me in, and the music, fight style, town development, and overall vibe kept me. it's like cozy game meets rouge-like with dark themes made cuter with by the adorable villagers and lamb. i've now purchased this game THREE times, once on PC with all the DLC's, once on PS5, and now on the switch as well. it has great long-term playability, and you can tell how much love the devs put into every bit of content they add.

overall, 10/10, excited to see what else these devs come up with! ♥

This game is perfect if you like a good cozy game mixed with some "Slay the Spire" dungeon mechanics which makes for great replay abilities. The followers praise you and it has some tycoon like qualities. You can choose to be peaceful, or not. 10/10 , 5 stars, and one of the best cult games. I would recommend it to anyone who like cute games with dark undertones occasionally.

Absolutely amazing game, I'd recommend to everyone who enjoy both worlds of management and roguelike. The art style is gorgeous, the music is definitely a classic.

My followers kinda pooped all over but being the leader of this silly little cult I will forgive them. (Don't tell them I'm sacrificing them later)
This is one of my favourite indie games of all time! Both parts of the game work so well and fun. Amazing art style! I played it both solo and coop and waiting to play new DLC. Really recommend!

this game's story art and gameplay is 10/10 the music though must be an 11/10 since i be humming along more than playing the game eventually killing my followers (don't worry i will just revive them from the darkest bit of hell)

Great game, super addictive loop of managing the cult and delving into dungeons. Everything felt very satisfying. Perfect mix of adorable and sinister in a really unique package. Despite the "roguelite" format it is quite reasonable to reach the end without spending a ton of time on it. The fact that time passes in your cult and problems multiply while you're away fighting in the dungeon can add some stress, but I didn't actually mind it too much and there's an option to change that in the settings if you really hate it. What else can I say, if this looks at all appealing to you then it's absolutely worth the playthrough.

At over 20 hours played, this game just keeps going and going with fun spins on the normal gameplay loop. I haven't gotten too deep into the DLC, but if it's anything like the base game it's gonna be a blast
this game is awesome, i love all the gameplay aspects its addicting
i got so excited to wed a perfect follower fresh from the egg and now i gain an understanding why cult leaders groom children. good game tho

One of the best rougelikes I've played. The combat feels smooth and responsive, where it's not rage-quit hard but its challenging. You're sure to die a couple times and it sucks but the game doesn't punish you too hard. The story is also super fun and creating your own cute little cult is so amazing. Sometimes I get so into the base building where I forget its a dungeon crawler! The art style is captivating and keeps my eyes on the game, and the little civilization management is created so well. I don't feel overwhelmed with the amount of things I want to do, but there's always constantly more things that I need to do. The game is very combat oriented towards the start and I feel shifts towards the base building mid-game, but I never get sick of one task. I end up playing for hours and realizing it's already night! 

I genuinely can't recommend this game enough, it's so ridiculously fun. Go buy it!!!

Cult of the Lamb is a fantastic colony sim and roguelite with lots of charm, unique mechanics and just difficult enough to be challenging, enjoyable and rewarding. 

You start with a small flock and along your journey to kill a bunch of godlike beings, you'll save more souls that'll join you to help build your settlement. 

All of the decisions you make will ensure your cult thrives and future dungeon runs are entered with you being more powerful.

Your cult is also there to be used as you please - want to play a "good" cult that celebrates followers ascensions and enjoying a holy-day? Sure! Want to nurture a cannibalistic cult that thrives on sacrifice and bloodshed? Also, sure! 

The DLCs are also worth picking up too! Woolfhaven being the major expansion, adding more content including a winter season for you to tackle, animal husbandry and more automation opportunities. 

9/10 game!
this game was good, but getting some of the achievements are nightmarish in terms of difficulty

I absolutely LOVED this game, the followers are adorable and watching them interact is so fun, each one has their own personality and I really grew to love them, the combat is clean and fun maybe a little repetative at times but overall an amazing experience
this game is much more fun then I initially thought. I never get bored. I think I could play it again and again, heheheh
Played it on a System, loved it, got it on PC just to remote play the DLC, Excited to check it out
Cult of the Lamb is one of the rare games that blends charm, chaos, and creativity into something unforgettable. It’s cute, it’s creepy, it’s hilarious, and it’s dangerously addictive.

very fun game!! combat flows really well and the boss fights are challanging (kallamar its on SIGHT with you) 

cult side of it it's very nice to decorate the main grinding is in the beginning. once you get the mine and lumberyard unlocked things get easier. 

WOOLHAVEN... woolhaven is my beloved thing. i love ynga so much and i love her arc. i have not finished the dlc yet but i plan to soon. 

leshy is my favorite bishop <3 chaotic ♥♥♥♥♥♥
this game is really chill. I often play it when I want to take a break, but on harder difficulties, it becomes a fun challenge. very fun, would reccomend

I love it, this game is amazing. At the time of this review im still going through woolhaven, but the base game and what ive seen so far of woolhaven is peak gaming. Ive sank so many hours into this game on my xbox, and now pc.
Really fun game. Good balance of fights and clan management. Kept me engrossed for hours. Soothing music, interesting theme. Really good game overall.
Mechanics and base arrangements are great. Randomizer of game is also good. In the fight, sometimes I felt I don't have the control on it. Game needs target lock in fights. Overall 9/10.
CULT OF THE LAMB is great because of how much freedom you get and how much the game gets updated, but I would like the packs to be cheaper. Other then that, it's a great game!

I love this game and in my opinion it's really fun. I mean it depends what style games you like but it's really fun to collect the little cult members and get to kind of design them, and there are some cool and interesting characters you can meet along the way. I would recommend this game. :)
I just bought ts yesterday and I'm already addicted to it

a follower pooped outside my tent.
so, naturally, i sacrificed him in a ritual
10/10 game

Animal Crossing Meets The Binding of Isaac: The Ultimate Cult Leader Simulator
Cult of the Lamb is a brilliantly twisted masterpiece that feels like a cute, cozy village builder wrapped inside a dark, satanic ritual. Developed by Massive Monster, this game asks a very simple question: What if Animal Crossing allowed you to sacrifice your neighbors to an ancient, eldritch deity? The result is one of the most addictive and beautifully cohesive gameplay loops in indie history.

What Makes It Legendary:
The Perfect Dual-Gameplay Loop: The game is perfectly split into two halves. By day, you go on adrenaline-fueled, roguelike crusades into chaotic dungeons to slice through heretics. By night, you return to your commune to build shelters, farm crops, and manage your growing cult. Both halves feed into each other flawlessly.

Unholy Rituals & True Power: You aren't just a leader; you are a god. You can marry multiple followers, force them to fast, throw them in jail for dissent, or sacrifice them in the middle of the night to instantly boost your own combat powers. The freedom to be a benevolent shepherd or a tyrannical despot is incredible.

The Visual Contrast: The art style is an absolute triumph. The contrast between the adorable, big-eyed animal cartoon designs and the grim, blood-soaked occult horror creates a unique aesthetic that is impossible to look away from. It is cute, cruel, and completely captivating.

Phenomenal Soundtrack: The audio design mixes catchy, whimsical village tunes with heavy, tribal, and eerie electronic beats during combat. It perfectly anchors the game's dual personality.

The Reality Check:
Late-Game Maintenance: Once your cult grows past 20+ followers, cleaning up their literal poop, cooking their meals, and managing their old-age deaths can occasionally feel like a hectic babysitting chore.

Combat Depth: While the combat is fast, responsive, and incredibly satisfying, it is slightly more casual and accessible compared to hardcore roguelikes like Hades or The Binding of Isaac.

The Verdict:
Cult of the Lamb is a flawlessly executed dark comedy. It takes the stress of a roguelike and cushions it with the cozy satisfaction of base building. It is a wonderfully twisted experience that proves you can be cute, evil, and highly profitable all at the same time.
10/10 - Praise the Lamb.
this is a great game but dificult of new players so dont rush everything

I'm highly addicted to this game. The loop of doing a quick dungeon run and then coming back to manage your cult just flows perfectly. The characters is so damn cute, some of them looks pretty dark at some point. Combat feels snappy and the progression keeps you hooked. Easily worth the price. Btw, Narinder is my favourite, The Lamb comes after :)

Such an amazing game! The "cute-meets-eldritch" aesthetic is perfect, and there is a huge variety of characters, bosses, and achievements that are actually incredibly fun to get. The combat is smooth, fluid, and fiercely satisfying.

The Woolhaven DLC intro absolutely slapped, bringing in a bunch of new bosses, characters, and an amazing soundtrack. Introducing harsh winter weather and base heat felt like a brilliant, cozy nod to Frostpunk, and the wolf raids added a nice layer of danger for livestock.

Honestly, I'd love to see this expanded further in the future since gameplay at the homebase eventually stagnates. Having heretics raid your cult directly, where your followers have to help defend it and you can craft them custom weapons and armor, would be an incredible addition. There could be 3 types of followers for defense: melee, ranged, and spellcasters/support (a Disciples-only class). Putting a simultaneous cap of a maximum of 12 active Disciples (perfectly preserving the "Apostles" achievement requirement) and letting them channel the Lamb's greatness via a mini skill tree with 2–3 active battle abilities. Having them fueled by accumulated, unused Devotion or some other magic resource that other followers generate while praying would add so much depth to the desciples and followers.

My only minor critique is that trying to "catch 'em all" and keep your unique, major characters (like Yngya) alive forever turns into a fulltime babysitting job (achievemnt perhaps?). I wish unique characters had a built-in exception to make them permanently immortal via the Altar, an extra quest, or some other way. Constantly reviving them because you run out of Golden Skull Necklaces is a major pain in the butt.

I just fully completed absolutely everything, and now I'm dealing with that classic post-game "feeling of loss" because I don't want it to end. I absolutely cannot wait for the next peak DLC.

First I would give a warning, as this game progresses to the later stages I found the gameplay loop fall into tedium, however the art and music was always good as well as the actual cult stuff. I didn't really enjoy the looking after the cult members though as most of the automation for the tedious things like food foraging and stuff was late game and required resources that where annoying to get. On top of this every time i did a run i would be punished for dying (I played on very hard) so i had to do a sermon and a bunch of other stuff to avoid a rebellion. Also i played with a half health double damage cloak and it really felt like there was a lot of gank though not to dark souls 2 level and in general i think the rougelite aspects were well balanced. Finally the side content was fun I enjoyed the dice game.

Tldr for anyone looking to buy i would say yeah if you like city builders, but if you just want the rougelite elements look for something a little different. But in general def a 7/10 game so above average but not outstanding (exept ovbs the art that is really cool)
Very addicting, great pacing, great gameplay loop. The game keeps you interested and I'm looking forward to the DLC.

One of my favorite games. The art is beautiful. The music is stellar. The lore is fascinating. 
RIP River Boy ♥️

Overall 10/10 100% would recommend playing the game.

If you like light management games but you get bored after a little a while of management then this game is perfect. The managing the cult is fun and not overwhelming. The combat is really satisfaction and doesn't get boring while playing normally. This game has become one of my favorites in a short time and I planning to 100% the base game then buying and 100%ing the dlc too.

If I HAD to say something negative then it would be how the boss difficulty curve falls off. SPOILERS [spoiler]After the 3rd boss Kallamar the other 2, Shamura and The One Who Waits, feel MUCH easier. It might be me but I was able to no hit Shamura after only 2 attempts while Kallamar took at least a week of attempts. And the I feel that The One Who Waits should be harder then it is[spoiler] but that's just me.

I bought this because the art style looked cute and I wanted something more relaxing to play.

A few hours later I was running a cult, sacrificing followers, and dealing with followers doubting my leadership.

Cult of the Lamb is way more addictive than I was expecting. The mix between managing your cult and going out on crusades works surprisingly well, and it always feels like there’s something to do.

The game also has a lot of personality. Everything looks adorable, but at the same time you’re doing completely insane things and somehow it just works.

I also got way too attached to some of my followers. I started naming them as a joke and then genuinely felt bad whenever one of them died. Thankfully I managed to get a few immortality necklaces to save my favorites.

10/10. Cute game about psychological manipulation.

There is always something to do or discover in this game. It gets addicting  pretty fast. I also like the blend between base management and the roguelike gameplay. The story is nice, the art style is cute and it feels weirdly cozy considering all the dark stuff the game is about. Recommended!

I have about 75 hours into this game and I haven't even finished my first save yet, I absolutely adore this game. I have about 50 followers and i have just started the woolhaven DLC after defeating all the bishops and healing them.
this game is so much fun and I've enjoyed every single second of it, and I don't usualy enjoy many games to this extent at all.

Spoiler free review of COTL and Woolhaven
Base game: 9/10
Woolhaven: 9.8/10
This game is amazing. The gameplay loop is really fun, the combat is fluid and challenging, especially post game, and it is overall a great game! Woolhaven is even better, adding a weapon so fun to play with I turned off all other weapons so I could just play with that one. I recently beat the postgame and the DLC, and I really enjoyed it! I only have a few complaints.
Pros: Combat, townbuilding, and story are great.
Cons: If you want to majorly rearange your base, it is difficult, as there is no way to temporarily remove things from the map. Also, there is a plot twist right before the final boss of Woolhaven that I think could have been built up to a bit more. 

Subcategories:
Combat: 10/10 Fluid and fast paced.
Town building: 9.3/10 Great, just difficult to do a full remodel.
Story: 9.5/10 Great story, but a bit unclear if you aren't searching for lore tablets. I recommend watching a full lore video after your first playthrough.
Postgame: 9.9/10 Absolutely the best part of the base game, only topped by Woolhaven.
Woolhaven: 10/10 I don't think they could have done better. They added things I didn't even know I wanted, but I can't play without now that I use them.
Graphics: 9/10 Great artstyle, and I have only come across two visual glitches. One is with the eyes of the Lamb occasionally glitch and the red becomes squares during certain rituals, and twice I have come across a layering issue, but that is nearly inevitable with a game like this. 
NPCs: 10/10 Each member of your cult has their own personality, likes, dislikes, and life. They act similar to an actual person. Very well programmed! 

Overall, this is an amazing game that I have played over 200 hours, and enjoyed pretty much all of it.

Note: I will update this review once I 100% the game.

There is always something to do in this game, or something that nudges your curiosity, which leads to very long sessions and a potential binge of the game.

This game, including all dlcs is a masterpeice and I don't think i've ever played a game so much after finishing the storyline.
Pretty fun ngl. I love the mix of managing a group of people and crusading.
Super fun and easy to get back into if you stop playing for a while (I've been playing this game on and off since it came out). + All the DLC (ON SALE) is worth it imo!

I love this game so much dude

Its my first time finishing a rougelike, and I will definitely be playing more games like this and keep returning to this masterpiece. 

Everything is amazing, the music, the combat, the art, and I never once felt the game was unfair at all. 

I'll do another playthrough when I get Woolhaven, if its peak this ♥♥♥♥ will go from a 9 to a 10
what a super good game. super funny, challenging but not too difficult. yes it has repetitive tasks but its not super annoying. has legend of zelda vibes. absolutely recommendable
ouu this game is PEAKED i just started playing but ITS ABSOLUTELY GOATED similar to animal crossing but this one get yo ball tickling highly recommend

Super fun and engaging. Has cozy game elements but is definately NOT a cozy game because you have to on crusades to further the game
Also, obsessed with Knucklebones now and am going to teach my family how to play it LOL
Great game, lovely graphics and great at being both cute and disturbing
Slept on this one for too long. Awesome blend of gameplay and progression mechanics, surprisingly versatile accessibility options. And it's so CUTE!
Cute graphics and nice game plot. I haven't game in years and am delighted that this is fairly easy to move along and rather addictive. Still at the early stages of the game, so would have to see.
it's a really fun game with tons of goofy jokes and deep lore. also simple controls and gameplay gimmicks that are easy to understand and make you think without being too confusing.
I have come back to this game multiple times with multiple updates, Sins of the Flesh, Woolhaven, etc.. It has always been a phenomenal joy from start to finish. All hail the one true lamb god <3

Once you get to mid to late game there is massive game breaking issues with lag that completely ruin the experience. Great game, but I cannot recommend a game that uses 30% of my GPU and can't crack 30FPS. 

I've seen some fixes online with adding some parameters to your boot file, but that didn't work for me. It seems to be something that has been long standing as these issues have threads online that backdate to release.
Fun game, a mixture of Stardew Valley with roguelike combat that can be super difficult if you want. It can be played in a more casual way or in a more punishing route. I liked it.

Game was a blast I waited for a sale to get it and I absolutely would've paid full price for it and its DLC's. The DLC's are a must. Mainly Wool haven but the others are nice touches. This quickly became my most played game at 210 hours.

Love playing this game. Did not know what to expect from it and what I got was fun, cuteness with chaotic entities trying to stop you. Gameplay was easy to pick up and made me work hard to make my cult the best it could be. If you like a twist on cozy games that make you work to make your place better, this is the game for you.

[b]Visual :[/b] The game looks stunning, it has sharp colors, animations are very fluid and the overall art direction is really well realized. The overly happy aesthetic of the camp clashes in a really good way with the overall vibe of the game. I really like the dichotomy of the look of your camps and the horror or the areas you explore in the rogue-like sections. 

[b]Gameplay :[/b] I was really impressed by how well made this game is. I was a bit worried of the two loops clashing, but the rogue-like and the "camp management" are well separated, while being complementary to each other. On top of that, both have really good depth and customization to fit one's gameplay style. 

While I played a good amount of hours on my first playthrough, I feel like I barely scratched the surface on all the mechanics tied to the followers and buildings. 

[b]Story :[/b] I found the little story fun and interesting. I felt that it lacks a bit of "lore drops" to tie everything together, but I think that it's something you get through 100% completion. I'll definitely pay extra attention to it when I revisit this game. 

[b]Audio :[/b] The music was really good and was fitting for both types of gameplay. The SFX were all nice and polished, nothing was annoying me. The little voices for the followers were really nice. 

[b]Playability :[/b] The controls were simple and intuitive. It's a really important point in a rogue-like that can become a bit of a bullet hell like this one because there's nothing worse than getting hit / dying and being persuaded it's not your fault. Even interacting with followers and managing the camp was really simple, which is good for how complex it can get. 

Very fun, looks amazing, everything works well and I'll be coming back for a second playthrough with the DLC. I give it a 4.5/5!

Cute, fun to plat and great animations. I just love this game so much and even after you finish the main story there's a lot more you can do (not talking about the DLCs but those are fantastic as well, can't wait to buy Woolhaven!!

Best 2.5D game I've played so far. Both the combat and base building in this game are very well built and entertaining. With a great soundtrack, an appealing cartoonish art style, and endless features to explore, this is easily one of the best games I've played by far.

Absolute banger of a game. Maybe not for everyone, but I personally loved this. I like the mix of settlement building and adventuring, and the story is awesome. Nothing more to say, just buy the game.
Great game, DLC are very fun too. Short if you don't care about achievements tho
This game is just super consistent fun. All aspects of it are polished and enjoyable,
I run a cult that sacrafices people and i love it 10/10 Love murdering people
i like the artwork and just the overall gameplay. the story is good and now im just saving up for the dlc.
this game is really unique and i really liked but it was getting very frusterating but all in all i loved this game i would recommend this game
Awesome game, love the lore and the characters a lot <3

I LOOVVEEE this game, it's so weirdly addicitve and would reccomend it to all of my friends no question. I bought all the DLC (besides the soundtrack) and couldn't leave it alone since, this game is exactly what it wants to be

This game is so cute and gory at the same time has a great amount of hard but balanced fights. i am playing on not a super high end device and it runs great this is the best game i have seen in 9 years

A lovely little roguelike with a neat base building activity. The rougelike part of the game can be made trivial with ingame settings for those who prefer to focus on expending theire cult.

good game, cute game. beginner friendly roguelike combat that's adjustable to whatever difficulty you want. this game is both silly and horrifying (a la happy tree friends lol), but totally loads of fun. half the time you're out on runs killing gods and foraging for materials and ♥♥♥♥, and the other half you're babysitting your lively cult full of a variety of weirdos. the art is fantastic, the music is cool af, the gameplay is super fun and the story is interesting! long live the lamb!!
All the animation's adorable, love to see the devs have fun with the projects as much as its fun to be an audience member partaking in it.

Fun little game. The farming and the cult system is novel and fun to do. The game doesn't overstay its welcome. I would recommend it when on sale.

Cult of the Lamb is a true masterpiece that blends cozy, beautiful art with dark, unsettling themes in a way that feels both inviting and haunting. The gameplay is top‑notch, seamlessly combining roguelike dungeon crawling where you battle powerful gods with cult management that challenges you to maintain order, faith, and resources among your followers. This dual structure keeps the experience fresh and engaging, and every choice. whether in combat or in doctrine feels impactful. The balance between charming visuals and sinister undertones, alongside mechanics that reward both strategy and skill, makes the game stand out as one of the most creative and polished indie titles in recent years.
ive 100% cotl and woolhaven and enjoyed every second of it, the bosses and cult building are fun and i am definetly gonna put 200 hours atleast into it

Cult of the Lamb is Not Just Another Indie Game. It’s an Absolute Masterpiece.

If you’re scrolling past this thinking, "Oh, it’s just another mashup of The Binding of Isaac and Animal Crossing," stop right there. You could not be more wrong. This game doesn't just borrow from those classics—it entirely evolves them.

As of today, May 18, 2026, I have sunk 36 hours into this masterpiece, and I can confidently tell you it is one of the greatest games I have ever played. Here is why you need to buy it right this second:

1. The Progression is Addictive (and Full of Surprises)
Just when you think you’ve seen what this game has to offer, it pulls the rug out from under you. You will constantly unlock unbelievable rewards and mechanics you didn't even know were possible. It keeps transforming, growing, and scaling up without ever losing its core magic.

2. Deep, Unexpected Management Mechanics
Think communal sleeping quarters are the smartest layout for your camp? Think again—you'll have to find out why for yourself. Ever dreamed of building a beautifully tailored, hyper-specific thematic temple? This game lets you do it. The customization and management strategy run incredibly deep.

3. Wild Combat & Endless Options
The weapon selections in this game are absolutely wild. The math here is simple: More Playtime = More Weapons = More Followers = MORE OPTIONS. It is incredibly easy to pick up and digest, yet it offers a massive variety of combat styles that keep every single run feeling fresh and chaotic.

The Bottom Line:
You will not regret purchasing this game for a single second. If you want a game that respects your time, constantly rewards your curiosity, and delivers pure, unadulterated joy, add this to your cart right now.
